questions.question: The uppermost layer over the earth's surface is called the:
questions.explanation: The crust is the thinnest of all the layers and forms the topmost surface of the earth.

questions.question: Which layer of the earth is the thinnest?
questions.explanation: The crust is only about 35 km on the continental masses and only 5 km on the ocean floors.

questions.question: The main mineral constituents of the continental mass are silica and:
questions.explanation: The continental crust is called 'sial' because it is mainly composed of silica (si) and alumina (al).

questions.question: The oceanic crust mainly consists of silica and:
questions.explanation: The oceanic crust is called 'sima' because it is mainly composed of silica (si) and magnesium (ma).

questions.question: Just beneath the crust is the ______, which extends up to a depth of 2900 km.
questions.explanation: The mantle is the middle layer of the earth, located directly below the crust.

questions.question: The innermost layer of the earth is the:
questions.explanation: The core is the deepest layer of the earth, with a radius of about 3500 km.

questions.question: The central core of the earth is mainly made up of nickel and:
questions.explanation: The central core is called 'nife' (ni - nickel and fe - ferrous/iron).

questions.question: Any natural mass of mineral matter that makes up the earth's crust is called a:
questions.explanation: Rocks can be of different colors, sizes, and textures, and they form the earth's crust.

questions.question: Rocks formed when molten magma cools and solidifies are called:
questions.explanation: Igneous rocks are also called primary rocks because they are the first to form from magma.

questions.question: Igneous rocks are also known as ______ rocks.
questions.explanation: They are called primary rocks as they are the source of other rock types.

questions.question: Rocks that are formed from sediments compressed and hardened over time are called:
questions.explanation: Sediments are transported and deposited by wind/water, and then they harden into layers of sedimentary rocks.

questions.question: The remains of dead plants and animals trapped in layers of rocks are called:
questions.explanation: Fossils are generally found in sedimentary rocks.

questions.question: Igneous and sedimentary rocks can change into ______ rocks under great heat and pressure.
questions.explanation: Metamorphic comes from the word 'metamorphose', which means to change form.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a metamorphic rock?
questions.explanation: Limestone changes into marble under great heat and pressure.

questions.question: The process of transformation of the rock from one to another is known as the:
questions.explanation: The rock cycle is a continuous process where one type of rock changes into another under certain conditions.

questions.question: Molten rock found inside the earth is called:
questions.explanation: Magma is the molten material beneath the earth's crust.

questions.question: When molten magma comes out on the earth's surface, it is called:
questions.explanation: Lava is actually fiery red molten magma coming out from the interior of the earth.

questions.question: Basalt is an example of which type of igneous rock?
questions.explanation: Extrusive igneous rocks form when lava cools rapidly on the earth's surface.

questions.question: Granite is an example of an ______ igneous rock.
questions.explanation: Intrusive igneous rocks form when magma cools slowly deep inside the earth.

questions.question: Sandstone is made from grains of:
questions.explanation: Sandstone is a sedimentary rock formed from the accumulation and compression of sand grains.

questions.question: Which of the following is used as fuel and found in rocks?
questions.explanation: Coal, natural gas, and petroleum are minerals found in rocks that are used as fuels.

questions.question: The Red Fort is made of which type of stone?
questions.explanation: The Red Fort in Delhi is a famous monument made of red sandstone.

questions.question: The Taj Mahal is made of:
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is a world-famous monument made of white marble (a metamorphic rock).

questions.question: What is the radius of the earth's core approximately?
questions.explanation: The innermost layer (core) has a radius of about 3500 km.

questions.question: Minerals are naturally occurring substances which have certain physical properties and definite ______.
questions.explanation: Every mineral has a specific chemical composition that distinguishes it from others.
